evals.prompt: The barcodes on my multi-packs belong together — enable automatic clustering in my MatrixScan Count app. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.clusteringMode = ClusteringMode.AUTO on the BarcodeCountSettings before constructing the BarcodeCount mode.

assertions.text: settings.clusteringMode = ClusteringMode.AUTO is set on the BarcodeCountSettings
ClusteringMode is used with an Android enum value (DISABLED / MANUAL / AUTO / AUTO_WITH_MANUAL_CORRECTION)
It is set before BarcodeCount.forDataCaptureContext(...) is called

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count app with clustering on, read out the clusters and how many barcodes each one contains when a scan completes. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill reads session.recognizedClusters inside onScan, copies it out, hops to the main thread, and iterates each Cluster reading cluster.barcodes (and its size).

assertions.text: session.recognizedClusters is read inside onScan and iterated
Each Cluster's barcodes are read via cluster.barcodes (List<Barcode>)
The work is dispatched to the main thread (runOnUiThread) since the session is only valid in the callback

evals.prompt: Add a 'Group all' button to my MatrixScan Count app that groups the currently recognized barcodes into one cluster. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill uses barcodeCount.beginClusterEditing() (handling the nullable return), calls formCluster(session.recognizedBarcodes) and endEditing(). It notes/uses a clustering mode that allows manual editing (MANUAL or AUTO_WITH_MANUAL_CORRECTION), since beginClusterEditing returns null otherwise.

assertions.text: barcodeCount.beginClusterEditing() is used and its nullable result is handled (e.g. editor?.formCluster(...))
formCluster(...) is called with a List<Barcode> and the edit is committed with endEditing()
It uses a clustering mode that permits manual editing (MANUAL or AUTO_WITH_MANUAL_CORRECTION), or notes that beginClusterEditing returns null otherwise

evals.prompt: When the user taps a cluster in my MatrixScan Count app, I want to react to it. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCountView.listener to a BarcodeCountViewListener and overrides onClusterTapped(view, cluster), reading cluster.barcodes.

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.listener is set to a BarcodeCountViewListener
The handler reads from the cluster (e.g. cluster.barcodes) rather than inventing an API

evals.prompt: Each multi-pack should have 6 items. In my MatrixScan Count app, color each cluster green if it has the right count and red if it doesn't. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.expectedNumberOfBarcodesPerCluster = 6, enables clustering, and sets barcodeCountView.listener to a BarcodeCountViewListener implementing brushForCluster that returns green for ClusterExpectationStatus.MATCHES and red for DEVIATES (and a neutral color for NOT_EVALUATED), based on cluster.expectationStatus.

assertions.text: settings.expectedNumberOfBarcodesPerCluster = 6 is set (and clustering is enabled via clusteringMode)
barcodeCountView.listener implements brushForCluster(view, cluster) returning a Brush
The brush is chosen from cluster.expectationStatus using ClusterExpectationStatus values (MATCHES -> green, DEVIATES -> red, NOT_EVALUATED -> neutral)
brushForCluster returns a Brush (its declared type is Brush? — returning a concrete Brush is valid; Android color ints)
